comment: N1b (+ ONT1a + ONT1b + AI1r additions) — manifest of common ontologies
pre-seeded into the internal neosemantics (n10s) semantic
repository on shepard startup. Currently eleven bundles (eight from
N1b plus the OBO Relation Ontology added by ONT1a, see
aidocs/58 §6, plus the NFDI4Ing metadata4ing ontology added by
ONT1b, plus the shepard-experiment domain ontology added by AI1r).
Each
entry pins the classpath-bundled file by SHA-256; the seed service
(de.dlr.shepard.context.semantic.OntologySeedService) refuses to
import a file whose hash drifted from this manifest. To refresh a
bundle, swap the .ttl file in and update its sha256 + size +
retrievedAt fields together.
These shepard-bundled files are minimum-viable Turtle stubs
carrying each ontology's canonical IRI prefix plus a handful of
representative classes/properties — enough for the casual
annotation flow on a fresh install. Operators who need the full
vocabularies replace the relevant .ttl with the canonical download
and regenerate this manifest; the N1c CLI
(`shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies`) automates that.
Entries are loaded in declaration order so cross-references (e.g.
dcterms used by schema-org and qudt) resolve correctly.
N1c2 — each entry carries a 'required' boolean. Required bundles
(prov-o and obo-relations today) are always seeded, even when an
operator names them in skip-bundles or runtime-disables them via
the admin endpoint. The PROV-O bundle backs the PROV1 audit-trail
and the Relation Ontology supplies the cross-cutting relations
(part_of / has_part / derives_from) the LUMEN-inspired showcase
and PROV-extending ontologies cite. See aidocs/65 §2.1.

ontologies.name: W3C PROV-O
ontologies.sourceRationale: W3C-hosted canonical Turtle serialisation of PROV-O; the W3C Document License permits redistribution alongside attribution.

ontologies.name: DCMI Metadata Terms
ontologies.sourceRationale: DCMI publishes the canonical DC Terms Turtle at the dublincore.org specifications path.

ontologies.name: schema.org core
ontologies.sourceRationale: schema.org publishes the current-https Turtle serialisation at version/latest; using https:// IRIs matches RO-Crate's canonical form.

ontologies.name: FOAF
ontologies.sourceRationale: Canonical FOAF spec is served as RDF/XML at xmlns.com; the shepard bundle ships a Turtle equivalent (n10s accepts either, the Turtle form is human-readable for cypher-shell inspection).

ontologies.name: QUDT Units of Measure
ontologies.sourceRationale: QUDT publishes per-vocab Turtle bundles under qudt.org/2.1/vocab/; unit.ttl is the units half and quantitykind.ttl the quantity-kinds half. Bundled together for shepard.

ontologies.name: OM-2 Ontology of Units of Measure
ontologies.sourceRationale: OM-2 publishes the canonical Turtle at ontology-of-units-of-measure.org/data/om-2.0/.

ontologies.name: W3C Time Ontology
ontologies.sourceRationale: W3C serves the Time Ontology under content negotiation at /2006/time; requesting application/turtle returns the canonical TTL serialisation.

ontologies.name: OGC GeoSPARQL
ontologies.sourceRationale: OGC publishes the GeoSPARQL ontology with content negotiation at opengis.net/ont/geosparql; the bundled Turtle equivalent ships alongside per the OGC Open Data Licence.

ontologies.name: OBO Relation Ontology (RO)
ontologies.sourceRationale: OBO Foundry serves the canonical RO at purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro.owl as OWL/RDF-XML; n10s accepts both serialisations and the bundled Turtle equivalent ships alongside for human-readable cypher-shell inspection. Added per aidocs/58 §6 (ONT1a) — RO supplies derives_from / has_input / has_output / part_of relations the LUMEN-inspired showcase cites.

ontologies.name: Metadata4Ing (NFDI4Ing)
ontologies.sourceRationale: NFDI4Ing publishes the canonical Turtle serialisation under the w3id.org permanent identifier with content negotiation; the 1.4.0 release (Dec 2025) is the current stable. Added per ONT1b — metadata4ing extends PROV-O for engineering-research provenance (m4i:ProcessingStep subtype of prov:Activity, m4i:Method, m4i:Tool, m4i:InvestigatedObject, m4i:NumericalVariable + QUDT-unit hookup) and unblocks the future `Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing` content-type on /v2/provenance/* (PROV1h).

ontologies.name: Shepard Experiment Ontology
ontologies.sourceRationale: Shepard-authored domain ontology (AI1r). Seven SKOS ConceptSchemes covering experiment life-cycle phases (with prov:Activity subclassing for PROV1-series provenance stamping), measurement roles, quality flags, defect types, inspection methods, manufacturing processes, and sensor roles. Designed for annotation of shepard DataObjects and References in engineering and materials-research contexts. The stub is self-contained and authoritative — no canonical upstream URL; operators extend via `shepard-admin semantic ontologies upload` (N1c2).
